title: Make Delegation Chain Depth Semantics Explicit and Agent-Readable
description:
The `max_chain_depth` field on delegations uses cumulative (absolute) depth counting from the root, not relative hops from the current delegator. An AI agent creating a delegation with `max_chain_depth=1` expecting to allow one further re-delegation will be surprised when the delegate immediately cannot re-delegate — because the current chain is already at depth 1.

This ambiguity causes silent misconfiguration: the delegation is accepted (201), but the intended behaviour never works. An agent has no way to detect this without attempting a re-delegation and observing a failure.

This task makes the semantics explicit and machine-readable:

1. **Add `chain_depth_remaining` to `DelegationResponse`**: Computed as `max_chain_depth - chain_depth`. This tells the delegate exactly how many more hops are allowed from their position. A value of `0` means the delegate cannot re-delegate.
2. **Add `depth_semantics` to the `/discover` response** under a `delegation` key:
   ```json
   {
     "delegation": {
       "depth_semantics": "absolute",
       "depth_semantics_description": "max_chain_depth is the maximum absolute depth from the root delegation. chain_depth_remaining shows how many further hops are permitted from the current delegate."
     }
   }
   ```
3. **Improve the chain-depth-exceeded error message**: When a delegation is rejected due to depth, include `chain_depth_remaining: 0` and `effective_max_chain_depth` in the error response body so the agent can log or adapt.
4. **Rename consideration (non-breaking)**: Add `max_chain_depth` alias `max_absolute_chain_depth` as a read-only field in responses (not required in requests) to make the semantics self-documenting.
validation: chain_depth_remaining is 0 when max_chain_depth equals chain_depth (delegate cannot re-delegate)
GET /discover includes delegation.depth_semantics_description explaining cumulative counting
Chain-depth-exceeded error body includes chain_depth_remaining and effective_max_chain_depth fields
DelegationResponse includes max_absolute_chain_depth as alias for max_chain_depth
Existing max_chain_depth field is preserved unchanged (non-breaking)
Integration test: delegation at depth 1 with max_chain_depth=1 returns chain_depth_remaining=0
Integration test: delegation at depth 0 with max_chain_depth=3 returns chain_depth_remaining=3
Integration test: rejected delegation error includes chain_depth_remaining=0 and effective_max_chain_depth
